objective: Create the canonical end-to-end Event Storming record for recalling project progress and continuing an eligible Task through required approval to an observable Execution result.
invariants: A Project resolves uniquely before ReportProjectProgress begins.
A ProjectProgressReport uses only Project-configured provenance-bearing sources and never hides missing sources or conflicts.
ProjectProgressReported occurs only after the user can observe the report.
A Task completes only after its acceptance criteria pass and no required human decision remains pending.
Delegated Execution is scoped, expiring, risk-ceiling-limited, and concurrency-limited and never bypasses R3 or R4 approval.
A worker may make the initial attempt plus at most three retries, but an uncertain external effect must be reconciled before retry.
DevelopmentPublishedForReview requires the expected remote commit and correctly targeted draft pull request to be verified.
decisions: The main scenario starts with ReportProjectProgress and continues with PublishDevelopmentForReview.
ReportProjectProgress is an R0 Command that starts a fresh observable verification Execution without changing project state.
Task is the Orchestration term; GitHub Issue is external Integration language and spec worktree is delivery-policy language.
Task uses Planned, Ready, Active, WaitingForReview, Completed, and Cancelled; Blocked is an eligibility condition rather than a state.
Delegated Execution is the canonical term; YOLO Mode may be a UI alias and DelegateTaskExecution is R2.
Mobile chat may embed a secure ApprovalRequest-bound card, but free-form chat text is not an approval decision.
Detailed trace and cost presentation is outside the MVP; minimum audit and diagnostic evidence remains required.
The product owner approved the conversational model and explicitly accepted the written canonical record on 2026-07-13 without requesting changes.
implementation_state: The product-owner Domain Discovery conversation resolved the task-execution actors, Commands, Domain Events, Policies, Hotspots, invariants, Task lifecycle, Published Language boundaries, failure scenarios, and acceptance evidence. The product owner accepted the written canonical record at my-docs/okf/event-storming/issue-12-task-execution-domain-flow.md. scripts/spec-worktree finish-feature 12 returned the worktree to spec/domain-discovery and deleted the reviewed feature branch. GitHub issue #12 is closed and its Project status is Done. No product code was implemented and no next issue was selected.
